title: Descaling Required
shortDescription: The machine needs descaling to remove mineral buildup.
fullDescription: The DESCALE indicator activates after a set number of brew cycles to remind you to descale. Mineral deposits from water accumulate inside the heating element and water lines, reducing performance and brew temperature.

causes.cause: Normal mineral buildup from regular use

symptoms: DESCALE message on display or dedicated LED illuminated
Machine makes more noise during brewing

fixSteps.title: Prepare the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the water reservoir. Remove any water filter. Do not insert a K-Cup pod.
tools: Keurig descaling solution or white vinegar

fixSteps.title: Fill with descaling solution
fixSteps.instruction: Pour the entire bottle of Keurig descaling solution into the reservoir, then add fresh water to fill to the MAX line.

fixSteps.title: Run descaling brew cycles
fixSteps.instruction: Place a large mug on the drip tray. Select the largest cup size and brew. Repeat until the ADD WATER indicator turns on, discarding the liquid each time.

fixSteps.title: Rinse cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Fill the reservoir with fresh water and run at least 12 large cup rinse brews to flush all descaling solution from the system.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Descaling is always a DIY task.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig descale light how to descale fix

faq.q: How often should I descale my Keurig?
faq.a: Every 3-6 months, or when the DESCALE indicator activates.

faq.q: Can I use vinegar instead of Keurig solution?
faq.a: White vinegar works but requires more rinse cycles (12+) to remove the taste. Keurig solution rinses more easily.

faq.q: Why won't the descale light turn off?
faq.a: You must complete the full descaling procedure including all rinse cycles. On some models, hold the 8oz and 12oz buttons simultaneously for 3 seconds to reset.

title: Machine Needs Priming
shortDescription: The water system has an air lock and needs to be primed.
fullDescription: The PRIME error occurs when the machine cannot draw water from the reservoir. This usually happens after the reservoir runs empty during brewing, after descaling, or when the machine has been stored without use.

causes.cause: Air lock in the water line after running dry
Debris blocking the water inlet needle

symptoms: Machine attempts to brew but no water comes out

fixSteps.title: Reseat the water reservoir
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water reservoir, empty it, and clean the connection area. Refill with fresh water and firmly reinsert until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Tap the reservoir
fixSteps.instruction: With the reservoir installed, gently tap the bottom to dislodge air bubbles near the intake valve.

fixSteps.title: Run water-only brew cycles
fixSteps.instruction: Without a K-Cup, run 2-3 water-only brew cycles on the largest cup size to push air through the system.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet needle
fixSteps.instruction: Check the water reservoir inlet for mineral buildup or debris. Clean with a paperclip or the Keurig maintenance tool.

whenToCallPro: If the pump has failed and cannot generate suction.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig prime error fix air lock reservoir

faq.q: What causes a Keurig to need priming?
faq.a: Running the reservoir dry introduces air into the water line. Always refill before it empties completely.

faq.q: Why does my Keurig say PRIME with a full reservoir?
faq.a: The reservoir may not be properly seated, or air bubbles are trapped near the intake. Remove, refill, and reseat firmly.

faq.q: Can a clogged needle cause PRIME?
faq.a: Yes, if the water inlet is partially blocked, the pump cannot draw water, triggering the PRIME error.

title: Water Reservoir Empty or Not Detected
shortDescription: The machine cannot detect water in the reservoir.
fullDescription: The ADD WATER indicator means the water level is below the minimum required for brewing, the reservoir is not properly installed, or the water level sensor is malfunctioning.

causes.cause: Water reservoir is empty or below minimum
Reservoir not properly seated on the machine

symptoms: ADD WATER message or indicator light
Reservoir appears to have water but not detected

fixSteps.title: Fill the reservoir
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the reservoir, fill to the MAX line with fresh water, and reinsert firmly.

fixSteps.title: Check the float valve
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the reservoir for the magnetic float. Shake gently to ensure it moves freely and is not stuck.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sensor area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the bottom of the reservoir and the sensor contact area on the machine with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Try without a water filter
fixSteps.instruction: If using a water filter, remove it temporarily to test if the filter is interfering with the float.

whenToCallPro: If the water level sensor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig add water error full reservoir fix

faq.q: Why does my Keurig say ADD WATER when it is full?
faq.a: The magnetic float may be stuck. Remove the reservoir, shake it gently, and reinsert.

faq.q: Can I use my Keurig without the water filter?
faq.a: Yes, the water filter is optional. It improves taste but is not required for operation.

faq.q: Why does the K-Mini show ADD WATER?
faq.a: The K-Mini has a small reservoir that must be filled for each cup. Fill to the desired cup line.

title: Machine Will Not Start Brewing
shortDescription: The machine powers on but will not start a brew cycle when the button is pressed.
fullDescription: The machine appears functional (lights on, display working) but pressing the brew button does not initiate a brew cycle. This can be caused by various issues from a clogged needle to a lid that is not fully closed.
symptoms: Brew button unresponsive or grayed out
Machine beeps but does not brew
Lights on but no brewing action
Cup size buttons do not respond

fixSteps.title: Close the handle firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open and firmly close the K-Cup handle until it locks. On some models, the handle must be in a specific position.

fixSteps.title: Clean the entrance and exit needles
fixSteps.instruction: The top entrance needle pierces the K-Cup. Use the Keurig needle maintenance tool or a paperclip to clear clogs from both needles.

fixSteps.title: Run a water-only cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the K-Cup and try running a water-only brew to clear the system.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 5 minutes and plug it back in. This resets the internal controller.

whenToCallPro: If the machine does not respond after needle cleaning and power cycling.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig not brewing fix needle cleaning

faq.q: How do I clean the Keurig needles?
faq.a: Open the handle, look inside for the top needle, and use a paperclip to clear the hole. The bottom needle is in the K-Cup holder.

faq.q: Why does my Keurig make noise but not brew?
faq.a: The pump is running but water cannot flow, likely due to a clogged needle or air lock.

faq.q: How often should I clean the needles?
faq.a: Every 1-2 months, or whenever you notice brewing issues.

title: Brew Stops Before Full Cup
shortDescription: The machine stops brewing before dispensing the selected cup size.
fullDescription: The machine begins brewing but stops before the cup is full, producing a partial or short brew. This is typically caused by a clogged needle, scale buildup, or air in the lines.

fixSteps.title: Clean both needles
fixSteps.instruction: Use the Keurig maintenance tool or a paperclip to clear both the entrance and exit needles.

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Run a full descaling cycle to remove scale buildup from the water lines and heating element.

fixSteps.title: Run water-only brews
fixSteps.instruction: Run 3-5 large cup water-only brews to flush the system.

fixSteps.title: Check the K-Cup
fixSteps.instruction: Some K-Cups with fine grind can clog the exit needle faster. Try a different brand or variety.

whenToCallPro: If short cups persist after thorough cleaning and descaling.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig short cup half brew fix needle descale

faq.q: Why does my Keurig only make half a cup?
faq.a: Clogged needles or scale buildup are the most common causes. Clean the needles and descale the machine.

faq.q: Do certain K-Cups cause short cups?
faq.a: Yes, very finely ground K-Cups can clog the exit needle faster. Try coarser ground varieties.

faq.q: Will descaling fix short cups?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. Scale restricts water flow, causing the machine to stop before a full cup is dispensed.

title: Machine Leaking Water
shortDescription: Water is leaking from the machine base, K-Cup area, or reservoir connection.
fullDescription: Water pooling under or around the Keurig indicates a leak from the reservoir connection, K-Cup holder area, or an internal hose. The source determines the severity.

causes.cause: K-Cup holder O-ring worn or damaged

symptoms: Drips from K-Cup area during brewing
Drip tray fills faster than normal

fixSteps.title: Check the water level
fixSteps.instruction: Do not fill above the MAX line. Overfilling causes water to spill when the reservoir is seated.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the K-Cup holder
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the K-Cup holder assembly and check the rubber O-ring seal. Clean or replace if worn.

fixSteps.title: Check reservoir seal
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the reservoir and inspect the seal or gasket where it connects to the machine. Clean and reseat.

fixSteps.title: Run a test brew
fixSteps.instruction: Place paper towels around the machine and run a brew to identify the exact leak location.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is from inside the machine body, indicating an internal hose issue.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig leaking water fix K-Cup holder seal

faq.q: Is water in the drip tray normal?
faq.a: A small amount of water in the drip tray after each brew is normal. Excessive water indicates a leak.

faq.q: Can I replace the K-Cup holder O-ring?
faq.a: Yes, replacement K-Cup holder assemblies include new seals and are easy to install.

faq.q: Why does my Keurig leak from the bottom?
faq.a: This typically indicates an internal hose issue or a cracked reservoir. Check the reservoir for cracks first.

title: All Indicator Lights On Simultaneously
shortDescription: All indicator lights are illuminated, preventing normal operation.
fullDescription: When all lights on a Keurig turn on simultaneously, it typically indicates a stuck button, a control board glitch, or the machine has entered a diagnostic mode.
symptoms: All cup size and indicator lights are on
Machine does not respond to any button

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine, wait 5 minutes, and plug it back in.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck buttons
fixSteps.instruction: Press each button firmly and release. Ensure none are stuck in the depressed position.

fixSteps.title: Drain and dry
fixSteps.instruction: If water was spilled on the machine, unplug it, empty the reservoir, and let it dry for 24 hours.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: On some models, hold the small cup and large cup buttons simultaneously for 5 seconds while the machine is plugged in.

whenToCallPro: If the lights remain after power cycling and checking for stuck buttons.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig all lights on fix reset

faq.q: Why are all lights on my Keurig on?
faq.a: Usually a temporary glitch. Unplugging for 5 minutes resolves most cases.

faq.q: Can water damage cause this?
faq.a: Yes, spilled water on the control panel can cause electrical shorts. Let the machine dry completely.

faq.q: Is this a sign my Keurig is broken?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Most all-lights-on situations are resolved with a power cycle.

title: Machine Not Heating Water
shortDescription: The machine dispenses water but it is cold or lukewarm.
fullDescription: The Keurig powers on and pumps water, but the water is not heated to the proper brewing temperature. This is caused by a failed heating element, scale buildup insulating the heater, or a thermal switch trip.

causes.cause: Heavy scale buildup insulating the heating element

symptoms: Coffee or water comes out cold
Machine seems to brew normally but liquid is not hot

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Heavy scale insulates the heating element. Run a full descaling cycle.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle to reset thermal switch
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes to allow the thermal safety switch to reset, then plug back in.

fixSteps.title: Run multiple hot water cycles
fixSteps.instruction: After descaling, run several large cup water-only cycles. The water should progressively get hotter.

fixSteps.title: Check for heating indicator
fixSteps.instruction: When powered on, the heating indicator should activate. If it never turns on, the heating element may have failed.

whenToCallPro: If the heating element has failed. Replacement is typically not cost-effective.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig not heating water fix cold coffee descale

faq.q: What temperature should Keurig brew at?
faq.a: Keurig machines heat water to approximately 192F (89C). Some models have adjustable temperature settings.

faq.q: Can I fix a broken Keurig heater?
faq.a: Heating element replacement requires disassembly and is often not cost-effective. Consider replacing the machine.

faq.q: Why does my Keurig make lukewarm coffee?
faq.a: Scale buildup is the most common cause. Regular descaling prevents this issue.

title: Coffee Grounds in the Brewed Cup
shortDescription: The brewed coffee contains visible coffee grounds or sediment.
fullDescription: Coffee grounds ending up in the cup indicates the K-Cup has been punctured incorrectly, the exit needle is damaged, or the K-Cup itself has a manufacturing defect allowing grounds to escape.

causes.cause: Using My K-Cup reusable filter with too-fine grind

symptoms: Visible coffee grounds in the cup
Grounds found in K-Cup holder after brewing

fixSteps.title: Check the exit needle
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the bottom exit needle in the K-Cup holder. Ensure it is straight and not enlarged. Clean any buildup around it.

fixSteps.title: Clean the K-Cup holder
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the K-Cup holder assembly and rinse it under running water. Clear any trapped grounds.

fixSteps.title: Try different K-Cups
fixSteps.instruction: Test with a different brand of K-Cup. Some brands have thinner foil that punctures poorly.

fixSteps.title: Adjust reusable filter grind
fixSteps.instruction: If using a My K-Cup reusable filter, use a medium-coarse grind. Too-fine grinds pass through the mesh.

whenToCallPro: If the needle mechanism is damaged and the holder cannot be replaced easily.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig coffee grounds in cup fix needle K-Cup

faq.q: Why are there grounds in my Keurig coffee?
faq.a: The exit needle may be enlarged or the K-Cup was not properly punctured. Clean the needles and try a different K-Cup brand.

faq.q: What grind size should I use in a reusable K-Cup?
faq.a: Medium to medium-coarse grind works best. Fine grinds will pass through the mesh filter.

faq.q: Are grounds in my coffee harmful?
faq.a: No, but they affect the taste and texture. Fixing the needle issue resolves the problem.

title: Touchscreen Display Not Responding
shortDescription: The touchscreen display is frozen, unresponsive, or blank.
fullDescription: On Keurig models with touchscreens (K-Supreme Plus SMART, K-Cafe SMART), the display may become unresponsive due to a firmware glitch, moisture on the screen, or a hardware fault.

causes.cause: Moisture or residue on touchscreen surface

symptoms: Touchscreen does not respond to touches
Display is frozen on one screen
Screen is blank but machine powers on

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 2 minutes and plug it back in.

fixSteps.title: Clean the screen
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the touchscreen with a dry, clean microfiber cloth. Moisture or coffee residue can interfere with touch detection.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Check the model manual for the factory reset procedure. On K-Supreme SMART: go to Settings > Factory Reset if the screen is partially responsive.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the machine to Wi-Fi and check for firmware updates through the Keurig app.

whenToCallPro: If the touchscreen hardware has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig SMART touchscreen not working fix reset

faq.q: Can I still brew with a frozen touchscreen?
faq.a: On some models, there are physical buttons that bypass the touchscreen for basic brewing.

faq.q: How do I factory reset my Keurig SMART?
faq.a: Navigate to Settings > Factory Reset in the touchscreen menu. If the screen is frozen, unplug for 5 minutes first.

faq.q: Does the Keurig app control the machine?
faq.a: The SMART models connect via Wi-Fi for firmware updates and some remote features.

title: Machine Does Not Power On
shortDescription: The machine shows no lights and does not respond to the power button.
fullDescription: The Keurig shows no signs of life when the power button is pressed. No lights, no sounds, and no display. This can be caused by a power supply issue, a tripped thermal safety switch, or an electrical fault.

causes.cause: Machine not plugged in or outlet has no power

symptoms: No lights when power button pressed

fixSteps.title: Check the power source
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the machine is plugged into a working outlet. Try a different outlet. Check the circuit breaker.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the power cord
fixSteps.instruction: Check the cord for damage, kinks, or burns. Ensure it is fully inserted into the machine.

fixSteps.title: Reset the thermal switch
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes to allow the thermal safety switch to cool and reset.

fixSteps.title: Try without the reservoir
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water reservoir and try powering on. Some models will not power on with a faulty reservoir sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the machine does not power on after checking all connections and resetting.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig not turning on no power fix dead machine

faq.q: Why did my Keurig suddenly stop working?
faq.a: The thermal safety switch may have tripped from overheating. Unplug for 30 minutes to reset.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing a dead Keurig?
faq.a: Depends on the model's value. Basic models may be cheaper to replace than repair.

faq.q: Can a power surge kill my Keurig?
faq.a: Yes. Use a surge protector to protect the machine from electrical surges.

title: Coffee Has Off-Taste or Bad Flavor
shortDescription: The brewed coffee has an unpleasant, plastic, or stale taste.
fullDescription: Coffee from the Keurig tastes off, plasticky, stale, or generally bad. This is commonly caused by mineral buildup, old water in the reservoir, or residual descaling solution that was not fully rinsed.

causes.cause: Mineral scale affecting water and coffee flavor
Stale water sitting in the reservoir for days
Residual descaling solution not fully rinsed

symptoms: Coffee has a plastic or chemical taste
Bitter taste unrelated to the coffee type

fixSteps.title: Change the water
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the reservoir completely, wash it with mild soap and water, rinse thoroughly, and refill with fresh filtered water.

fixSteps.title: Run rinse cycles
fixSteps.instruction: Run 6-8 large cup water-only brew cycles to flush the internal water lines.

fixSteps.title: Clean the K-Cup holder
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the K-Cup holder assembly and wash all parts in warm soapy water. Old coffee oils accumulate and become rancid.

fixSteps.title: Descale if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If the machine has not been descaled in 3+ months, run a full descaling cycle followed by 12+ rinse brews.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Bad taste is always a cleaning issue.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig coffee tastes bad plastic fix clean

faq.q: Why does my Keurig coffee taste like plastic?
faq.a: New machines can have a plastic taste. Run 10+ water-only brews before first use. On older machines, it indicates the need for thorough cleaning.

faq.q: How often should I change the reservoir water?
faq.a: Change the water daily or at least every 2-3 days. Stagnant water breeds bacteria and affects taste.

faq.q: Does the water filter improve taste?
faq.a: Yes, Keurig charcoal water filters reduce chlorine and mineral taste. Replace every 2 months.
